title: NEXUS-style skill specification stage added to LeGPS
summary: After checking Appendix F of NEXUS-1, LeGPS now has an optional explicit skill/reward decomposition stage. This implements the useful NEXUS idea of asking an LLM for skills, reward terms, anti-shortcut notes, and skill-selection rules before policy synthesis, without adopting the full NEXUS architecture.
motivation: NEXUS first queries an LLM for task-relevant skills and associated reward functions using the game manual and object-centric observation schema.
NEXUS then queries the LLM for a fixed skill selector or meta-policy.
LeGPS previously encouraged skill-like helper functions and dense_reward(...), but it did not save a separate reusable skill specification before policy generation.
implemented_changes: Added --skill-spec-mode off|llm to the single-game outer loop.
Added --refresh-skill-spec to regenerate skill_spec.json when needed.
When enabled, the loop asks the LLM for a structured JSON skill specification before the first policy is generated.
The skill specification is saved as skill_spec.json in the run directory and the raw LLM exchange is saved under llm_io.
Initial, diagnostic, diagnostic-rewrite, and improvement prompts now receive the saved skill specification as context.
The unified suite runner can forward --skill-spec-mode and --refresh-skill-spec to each game run.
Optimization results now record the skill_spec_mode and skill_spec_path.
what_this_does_not_claim: This is not a full NEXUS implementation.
LeGPS still uses one generated interpretable Python policy and CMA-ES over numeric parameters, not separately trained neural skill policies.
No new performance result is claimed yet from the skill-spec stage.
The final evaluation metric remains real environment reward.

rq_mapping.RQ1: May improve policy generation on sparse or multi-stage games by making required skills explicit before code generation.
rq_mapping.RQ2: Provides a better audit artifact for checking whether generated behavior matches intended skills or exploits shortcuts.
rq_mapping.RQ3: Creates a reusable skill decomposition that can later support task-variation adaptation.
rq_mapping.RQ4: Adds a future ablation axis: standard LeGPS versus LeGPS with an LLM-generated skill specification.

next_steps: Run a small Kangaroo or Seaquest single-game smoke with --skill-spec-mode llm.
Inspect skill_spec.json before interpreting any policy result.
If objective_mode is llm_shaped or hybrid, verify dense_reward(...) follows the saved skill reward terms and does not reward shortcuts.
Only promote skill-spec results after standardized real-reward evaluation and video/logger checks.
